title: stillingia prostrata
intro: Stillingia prostrata, also known as Queen's root or Indian turmeric, is a perennial plant native to North America, particularly in the southeastern United States and Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of stillingine, a unique alkaloid compound. The medicinal properties of Stillingia prostrata include its ability to reduce inflammation, combat bacterial and fungal infections, and even alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other joint-related issues. Herbal preparations of Stillingia prostrata can be made into teas, tinctures, or capsules, and have been used to promote overall health and well-being, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues and skin conditions.
